## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowder with a video about other webinar registration page options to test. So I'm constantly testing some interesting things come up from that. And so I'm going to show you two other interesting options here. Now neither one of these is unique to me. They're ideas that have come from other places, but I want to show you them because I'm finding that they're working in my testings. The first one is what I call the bare page. The second one is the opt-in incentive to webinar registration funnel. Let me describe each of these, the bare page option. Let's the add or email for people who are already on your list, do all the selling of what's in the webinar itself. Basically, it only has four elements, an image that illustrates the benefit that they're getting a solution based headline like lose five pounds in five days guaranteed, a countdown timer, and the registration button. That's it. So just an example of what this might look like. Free webinar reveals how to lose five pounds in five days guaranteed countdown timer button. That's it. Now there's a couple of things going on here. The first one is that you can allow your other elements that surround this particular page do the selling for you, your ads, your emails, etc. But the other piece that I want to point out to you that's key is that this page actually doesn't even need those to support it. You've got a compelling promise, a promise of a webinar to deliver that promise, and a button to sign up. That's it. Not a lot of fancy stuff, not a lot of explanation is needed. It's simply here's the promise, come sign up. It's getting some interesting results. The next option is the opt-in incentive to webinar registration funnel. So I've mentioned this before. It's specifically designed for ad based cold traffic, which basically gives them something before asking for a commitment. So one of the challenges of bringing in cold traffic is we're bringing in a group of people who don't know you, don't trust you, and you're immediately asking them to commit to giving you an hour and a half of their time to learn things from you. And so putting an opt-in incentive before that tends to build that trust. And it also captures their email address before asking for webinar registration. So the 70% who won't sign up for a webinar, it gets their email address. So you're not wasting those ad dollars for that group of

## [03:02 - 06:03]

people. So basically here's how the funnel works. An ad featuring an opt-in offer. From there, they go to the opt-in offer itself. And then they get the webinar registration page. So the webinar registration is the opt-in pages, thank you page. So they see the offer, they opt in, and then the thank you page is says something about check your email in 10 minutes, it'll be here in the meantime, sign up for this webinar. Keys to success with this. The first one is it's got to have a compelling opt-in offer. Second off is the webinar needs to be a logical continuation of that opt-in offer. So it needs to build on what we just promised. So the opt-in offer gives you X. The webinar gives you X plus this. And then the webinar registration page acknowledges the fact that they've just opted in for something and gives them a logical reason why they should attend. So it might be that the opt-in offer teaches them what to do. And then the webinar is going to show you step by step exactly how to do it. Or on the webinar, I'm going to be coaching you on how to do this, answering your specific questions. One of the keys to success here is that the better you can do at explaining why what you'll show in the webinar is better than what they're going to get in the report, the better off your webinar is going to do. So let me show you a couple of examples of how you might do this. So let's say, for example, that your opt-in, the ad sends them to a personality quiz. Then you have an opt-in incentive that gives them their results. And then you offer a webinar that shows them how to apply those results in their life. By the way, this very exact thing you're seeing here on the screen is the core of two separate evergreen funnels that I know of that are working incredibly well. So it starts with the quiz, opt-in to get the results, a webinar that explains how to apply those results in their lives. Another example, so how to create a killer scholarship essay. So an opt-in incentive teaches how to write that webinar, how to write that essay. And then the webinar coaches five people on theirs. Here's another one. How to find house flipping opportunities in

the MLS database. So the online database, when you go to a real estate site and it shows you houses in your area, that's called MLS, the multiple listing service system. I can't remember what it is. So this is what you're promised is you're going to show them how to find house flipping opportunities in that database. So the opt-in incentive shows them the actual criteria to look for. And then the webinar shows them a live demonstration of how we identify potential flipping opportunities online from home in our jammies. So you're actually going to go live and go look through the database and actually find things their lives so they can see how it all works. Now, sometimes, often, you actually need a video to connect the logic of why a webinar is needed or advantageous. So that thank you page from the opt-in offer, then sometimes needs a video on it to explain this is why we're holding this webinar. Not always. All depends on how clear your transition is from what you gave them in the free opt-in offer is to what you're going to offer incrementally in the webinar. The key is you're giving them something incremental in the webinar. And the reason for that incremental is what gets people to sign up for your webinar. I encourage you to be willing to test different registration pages and funnels as you go through this entire process. Because remember, we're not just doing a webinar here.
